title: Heaven Defying Cultivator
content:
“The heavens’ dao won’t allow a third type of heaven-defying cultivation… Countless years ago, when the universe first formed, there was a race that was as old as the heavens’ dao. The members of that race had a lot of power. They used that power to form their dao, and they used that dao to fight the heavens. Some had amazing spells; with a turn of their hand, they could destroy dao and use the spells to fight against the heavens. There were also those that walked the path of slaughter; they lived in slaughter and used death to fight against the heavens!

“They called themselves the Ancient Order!

“Divine retribution wasn’t able to destroy their bodies, and the heavens’ might wasn’t able to make them yield. They became the first lifeforms that cultivated while defying the heavens...

“After countless years, the Ancient Order split apart! Those with extreme power called themselves ancient gods… Those that walked the path of slaughter called themselves ancient devils… Those that controlled powerful spells called themselves ancient demons! They were the second lifeforms that cultivated while defying the heavens!”

The third lifeform that cultivated while defying the heaven are a very small part of humankind.

Heaven-defying cultivation represents a path in the cultivation world that fundamentally challenges and resists the natural order and laws established by the heavens or the universe. This type of cultivation is defined by rebellion against divine or cosmic laws, often incurring severe consequences or divine retribution, but it grants cultivators unique and extraordinary powers.
Heaven-defying cultivators refuse to submit to the established order of the heavens, seeking to surpass or even break the limitations imposed by natural laws, divine will, or reincarnation cycles. They are often seen as anomalies or “weeds” in the cosmic design, carving out their own path rather than following the heavens’ dao. Because of this defiance, divine retribution is a constant threat, though some cultivators, such as Wang Lin, manage to resist or even destroy such retribution, reclaiming their own fate and uncovering hidden truths about the world. Unlike traditional cultivation, which emphasizes understanding and following the heavens’ dao, heaven-defying cultivation involves pursuing a personal dao, advancing through stages.
